title: sorghum halepense subvar. submuticum
intro: Sorghum halepense subvar. submuticum, also known as Johnson grass, is a type of perennial grass native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to reduce fever, relieve pain, and protect against cell damage. The health benefits of Sorghum halepense subvar. submuticum include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs.
